common.submissionProfileIncomplete: Operator profile incomplete — territory not assigned
common.stepNotImplemented: This step has not been implemented yet.

hero.eyebrow: The first regenerative map of tourism
hero.heading: Travel impact. Visible at the moment of booking.
hero.body:
We're building something that's never existed in tourism — a live impact layer that tells what tourism does to places, people and communities.

For operators ready to measure and improve — a way to be found by travelers who care. For travelers — a way to make every trip count.

manifesto.heading: There will be no tourism industry on a dead planet.
manifesto.subheading: We keep talking about sustainable travel while the places we love quietly disappear. The problem isn't that travelers don't care. The problem is that caring has never been enough.
manifesto.body: You can want to do the right thing and still have no way to know if you are. You can be an operator doing everything right — and still lose to someone who isn't. Because the system wasn't built to tell the difference. We're building that system.

foundingCohort.heading:
30 operators.
One island.
The first regenerative map
in tourism history.

experiencePreview.eyebrow: Coming soon — Off the beaten track.
experiencePreview.heading: This is what booking looks like when impact is visible.
experiencePreview.body: Local Madeiran guide running electric-car tours to places most visitors never reach. 100% local operator, zero direct emissions, low-pressure destinations.
experiencePreview.caption: 100% electric · 100% local

meetTheScore.heading: Your verified impact score — visible at the moment of booking.

footprint.title: Operational Footprint
footprint.desc: How light is your operation on the planet? Energy, water, waste and carbon — measured against real data, not estimates.

local.title: Local Integration
local.desc: How much of what you earn stays here? Local jobs, local suppliers, direct bookings.

regen.title: Regenerative Contribution
regen.desc: What do you actively give back? Your own programmes, community initiatives, or institutional partnerships — as long as it's real and traceable.

meetTheScore.cta: Your Green Passport Score — verified and live at booking.

howItWorks.heading: Here's what happens when you apply

assessment.title: Complete your assessment
assessment.desc: Answer questions about your real operations — energy, water, local employment, what you give back. No estimates unless that's all you have. No greenwashing.

verify.title: We verify
verify.desc: Our team reviews your data before anything goes live. Not automated. Not instant. Rigorous on purpose.

live.title: Your score goes live
live.desc: Visible to travelers at the moment they book. Everything you do right — finally visible to the people looking for exactly that.

dpi.heading:
Context matters.
We measure that too.
dpi.body:
The Destination Pressure Index contextualises your score against where you operate — tourist intensity, ecological sensitivity, economic leakage.

Operating responsibly here isn't abstract. It's urgent. And now it's measurable.

forwardCommitment.heading:
Low score today?
That's a starting point.
forwardCommitment.body: The Green Passport doesn't penalise — it maps where you are. Sign a Forward Commitment, receive 15 baseline Pillar 3 points, and get matched with verified local institutions.

whatIs.question: What is The Regenerative Tourism platform?
whatIs.answer: A verification layer for tourism. We measure and make visible the real environmental and social impact of operators — at the moment of booking.

greenPassport.question: What is the Green Passport?
greenPassport.answer: Your verified impact identity as a tourism operator. A score from 0 to 100 across three pillars, updated each cycle, visible to travelers when they book.

different.question: How is this different from sustainability certifications?
different.answer: Most certifications are static and hard to compare. We use a dynamic scoring system designed to be visible, transparent, and continuously updated.

verification.question: How does the verification process work?
verification.answer: Operators complete an assessment, submit supporting data, and receive a verified score. The process is structured, but designed to be simple.

lowScore.question: What if my score is low?
lowScore.answer: That's a starting point. We provide Insights and guidance to help improve over time. The goal is progress — not perfection.

join.question: How can I join?
join.answer: You can apply to be part of the founding cohort or start the free assessment to understand your impact.

footer.tagline: A verification layer for tourism — making impact visible at the moment it matters most.
footer.copyright: © {year} The Regenerative Tourism. All rights reserved.

pricing.subtitle: Every operator can complete the Green Passport assessment for free. Publication on the regenerative map and verified scoring require a subscription.

free.subtitle: Complete the full assessment at no cost

features.one: Full assessment across all three pillars
features.two: Internal score visible only to you
features.four: Score not published on the map

founder.subtitle: First 30 operators — Madeira cohort
founder.notice: Limited to 30 spots — 3 months free to start

features.two: Published score on the regenerative map

standard.subtitle: For operators joining after the founding cohort

features.two: Published score on the regenerative map

global.title: We’re launching in Madeira — and going global.
global.body: The founding cohort of 30 operators in Madeira will establish the first verified regenerative dataset. After this initial phase, the assessment and verification layer will open to operators worldwide.

one.q: Can I really do the assessment for free?
one.a: Yes. The full assessment across all three pillars is completely free. You'll see your internal score and receive recommendations to improve.

two.a: Published operators appear on the regenerative map, get a public Green Passport page shareable with guests, and can embed a verification widget on their own website.

three.q: What happens after the 3-month free period for founders?
three.a: Founding operators transition to €29/month — a rate locked permanently. This includes ongoing verification, field documentation, and Pillar 3 guidance.

four.q: I’m not in Madeira — can I still join?
four.a: Absolutely. Any operator worldwide can complete the assessment today. Once we expand beyond the founding cohort, you can subscribe at the standard €60/month rate to publish your score.

destinations.title: Destination pressure index
destinations.subtitle: Every destination has a measurable pressure score — combining tourist intensity, ecological sensitivity, economic leakage, and regenerative performance.
destinations.comingSoonBody: Each territory will receive a scientifically computed DPI based on regional data from official statistics offices, conservation agencies, and economic research.

gstc.title: GSTC Framework Alignment
gstc.subtitle: The Global Sustainable Tourism Council (GSTC) establishes the global baseline standards for sustainable tourism. The Green Passport Score methodology aligns with and extends GSTC criteria — converting qualitative requirements into quantitative, comparable metrics while adding the regenerative contribution dimension that goes beyond compliance.

sdgs.title: UN Sustainable Development Goals
sdgs.subtitle: Tourism is the only economic sector explicitly named in three United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDG 8.9, SDG 12.b, SDG 14.7). The three pillars of this framework map directly and verifiably onto five SDGs.

hero.title: Verified Regenerative Operators
hero.description: Every operator listed here has been independently assessed across three pillars of regenerative impact. Scores are computed, not claimed.
hero.count: {count, plural, one {# operator with published GPS scores} other {# operators with published GPS scores}}

empty.title: No operators found
empty.description: Try adjusting your search or filters.

p1.label: Operational Footprint
p1.description: Energy, water, waste, carbon, and land use intensity per activity unit.

p2.label: Local Integration
p2.description: Employment, procurement, revenue retention, and community engagement.

p3.label: Regenerative Contribution
p3.description: Active ecological, cultural, or scientific contribution with institutional traceability.

detail.dpsDescriptionPost: — measuring how fast and consistently they improve across assessment cycles.

forwardCommitment.title: Forward Commitment — Pillar 3 In Development
forwardCommitment.description: This operator has formally committed to establishing a regenerative contribution programme.

detail.dpiDescription: Destination Pressure Index measures the ecological and social pressure this destination is under.
detail.dpiHighImpact: Choosing a high-GPS operator in a high-pressure destination is the highest-impact booking choice.
detail.evidenceCount: {count, plural, one {# item verified} other {# items verified}}
detail.evidenceNote: Indicator evidence has been reviewed and verified by a TRT assessor. Tier 1 = primary source documents · Tier 2 = secondary data · Tier 3 = institutional partnership records.
detail.auditDescription: This score is computed by the TRT Scoring Engine — a deterministic, stateless computation layer. Any GPS can be independently reproduced by passing the source AssessmentSnapshot and MethodologyBundle to the engine. Scores displayed here are read from persisted ScoreSnapshots, never computed on-the-fly.

hero.title:
How we measure
regeneration
hero.description: Three questions no booking platform can answer: How lightly does this operator touch the land? Does the money stay local? And is the place getting better because they're here?

p1.title: Operational Footprint
p1.question: How lightly does this operator touch the land?
p1.explanation: We measure five things every tourism operation uses: energy, water, waste, carbon emissions, and land. For each, we calculate how much the operator uses per guest per night — and compare it to clear benchmarks. The less you consume, the higher you score.

energy.desc: Electricity and fuel per guest per night — and how much comes from renewables.

water.desc: Litres consumed per guest per night. Bonus for renewable harvesting, greywater reuse, or treatment.

waste.desc: Percentage diverted from landfill — recycled, composted, or otherwise recovered.

carbon.desc: Carbon footprint per guest per night from on-site energy use.

site.desc: Integration with natural surroundings — biodiversity, landscaping, ecological sensitivity.

p2.title: Local Integration
p2.question: Does the money stay in the community?
p2.explanation: Tourism can either extract wealth from a place or reinvest in it. This pillar measures how deeply an operator is embedded in the local economy — through hiring, sourcing, ownership, and community ties.

employment.desc: Local staff percentage, fair wages, permanent contracts, working conditions.

procurement.desc: Food, supplies and services purchased from local businesses.

revenue.desc: Revenue retained locally — direct bookings and local ownership.

community.desc: Active engagement — partnerships, shared resources, cultural activities.

p3.title: Regenerative Contribution
p3.question: Is this operator actively improving the place it depends on?
p3.explanation: "The hardest pillar." It asks whether the operator goes beyond "doing less harm" and actually contributes to restoring ecological, cultural, or scientific capacity. Verified, traceable, additional.

scope.desc: Areas covered — ecological restoration, cultural preservation, research, community capacity.

traceability.desc: Can the contribution be traced to a specific outcome? A tree, a species, a paper.

additionality.desc: Does the programme create something genuinely new — not relabelling existing activity?

touristIntensity.label: Tourist Intensity
touristIntensity.friendly: How many visitors relative to the local population? Small islands with millions of tourists face more pressure.

ecologicalSensitivity.label: Ecological Sensitivity
ecologicalSensitivity.friendly: How fragile is this place? Protected areas, forest coverage, marine reserves — places that need careful stewardship.

economicLeakage.label: Economic Leakage
economicLeakage.friendly: How much tourism revenue stays in the destination — versus flowing out to foreign chains and global platforms?

regenPerformance.label: Regenerative Performance
regenPerformance.friendly: How well are local operators already performing? Low scores across the board mean more destination pressure.

t1.label: Direct evidence
t1.desc: Meter readings, utility bills, invoices, payroll records.

t2.label: Supporting documents
t2.desc: Supplier certificates, third-party reports, audit documentation.

t3.label: Self-reported
t3.desc: Operator declarations — accepted but weighted lower, flagged for verification.

proxy.label: Proxy calculation
proxy.desc: Validated estimation methods with a built-in correction factor.

hero.label: Community
hero.title: Impact Record
hero.description: Travelers ranked by cumulative Choice Score — earned through verified regenerative bookings, activities, and check-ins.

comingSoon.title: Coming soon
comingSoon.description: The traveler leaderboard will be available once traveler accounts launch. Earn points by booking verified operators, checking in via QR code, and joining regenerative activities.

hero.label: Discover
hero.title: Verified regenerative operators
hero.description: Browse operators with verified Green Passport Scores. Filter by territory, band, or type — and find operators doing real work.

welcome.title: Welcome to The Regenerative Tourism
welcome.subtitle: Let's build your Green Passport
welcome.description: This assessment takes about 20–30 minutes. You can save and return at any time.
welcome.buildDescription: We'll walk you through 4 sections about your operation. It takes about 20 minutes — save anytime and come back where you left off.
welcome.autosave: Your progress is saved automatically. You can leave and pick up where you left off.
welcome.nda: Your data is protected under our NDA. Only aggregated, anonymised scores are ever shared publicly.

p1.label: Pillar 1 — Operational Footprint (40%)
p1.desc: Energy, water, waste, carbon & land use

p3.label: Pillar 3 — Regenerative Contribution (30%)
p3.desc: How you actively improve the place you depend on

navigation.tickConfirm: Tick the confirmation above to submit.

privacy.nda: Your data is protected under our NDA. Only aggregated, anonymised scores are ever shared publicly. Individual operational data remains confidential.

evidenceTier.sourceLabel: What is the source of this data?

operatorType.title: What type of operator are you?
operatorType.subtitle: Select the category that best describes your operation.
operatorType.stepTitle: What type of operation do you run?
operatorType.stepSubtitle: This determines which questions we'll ask you. Accommodation operators are benchmarked per guest-night, experience operators per visitor-day.
operatorType.accommodationDesc: Hotel, guesthouse, hostel, BnB, glamping, eco-lodge, surf lodge
operatorType.experienceDesc: Day tours, workshops, guided activities, photography tours, boat trips
operatorType.combinedDesc: Both accommodation AND experiences — scored using accommodation bounds
operatorType.accomGateQuestion: Does your operation include any overnight accommodation that guests pay for?
operatorType.accomGateWarn: It looks like you may be a combined operator. Please select Type C (Both).
operatorType.accomGateSelectC: Go back and select Type C
operatorType.infoBox: This choice affects all scoring benchmarks. Accommodation operators are measured per guest-night; experience operators per visitor-day with lower thresholds.

identity.title: Tell us about your operation
identity.stepSubtitle: These details are required for publication but are not scored.
identity.name: Business name
identity.descriptionPlaceholder: Describe your operation in a few sentences
identity.noDpiWarning: We do not yet have a DPI calculated for this destination. The current calculation uses Madeira as a reference territory, so the results may not fully reflect your local market.

ownership.title: Ownership
ownership.confidential: This information is confidential and protected under our NDA
ownership.livesLocally: Do you live within the destination region?
ownership.localEquity: % of equity owned by local residents
ownership.chainToggle: Part of a hotel chain or group
ownership.chainNamePlaceholder: Name of the chain or group
ownership.stepSubtitle: Understanding who owns your business and how it's operated.
ownership.howStructured: How is the business legally structured?
ownership.localEquityHint: Percentage of equity held by residents within 50 km of your operation.
ownership.partOfChain: Part of a chain or group?
ownership.soloHint: If yes, employment metrics default to 100% and you can skip employment fields.

accommodation.stepSubtitle: Tell us about your accommodation — this helps us set the right benchmarks.
accommodation.propertyTypeHint: Select the category that best describes your property.
accommodation.foodServiceHint: What F&B service do you provide to guests?

experience.stepSubtitle: What kind of experiences or tours do you offer? Select all that apply.
experience.selectAtLeastOne: Select at least one experience type.

activity.title: Operational activity
activity.stepSubtitle: The scale of your operation. These figures normalise your intensity metrics per guest.
activity.guestNightsHint: Sum of nights sold to guests over the 12-month assessment period.
activity.visitorDaysHint: Number of individual day-visits / participations in your experiences.
activity.revenueSplitAccomHint: Proportion of total annual revenue from accommodation (must sum to 100% with experience).

operationActivity.stepSubtitle: Tell us about your operation and its annual activity.
operationActivity.guestNightsCalcBody: Guest-nights = number of guests × number of nights stayed. For example, 2 guests staying 3 nights = 6 guest-nights.
operationActivity.foodOnSiteTooltip: This helps us tailor the procurement section — if you don't serve food, we won't ask about food & beverage sourcing.
operationActivity.foodAgriTipBody: Operators with on-site kitchens can significantly boost their P2 score by sourcing from local farms and producers within 100 km. We'll ask about this in the procurement section.
operationActivity.visitorDaysTooltip: Count each participant per day. Half-day and short sessions are weighted fractionally.
operationActivity.visitorDayDef: Visitor-day = 8 hours of activity per participant.
operationActivity.visitorDayCalc: Full day (8h) → 1.00 · Half day (4h) → 0.50 · Short session (2h) → 0.25

photos.title: Add photos
photos.stepSubtitle: Upload photos that travelers will see. The first photo becomes your cover image.
photos.subtitle: Upload up to 6 photos of your operation.
photos.dragDrop: Drag and drop or click to upload
photos.guidelineResize: Large images are automatically resized to 1920px max
photos.guidelineFormat: JPEG, PNG, or WebP — max 10MB per file
photos.guidelineDashboard: You can add or change photos anytime from your dashboard
photos.photoTip: Don't have professional photos? No problem — share what you have and we can help you create polished visuals for your public profile.

energy.title: Energy
energy.stepSubtitle: Indicator 1A · Weight: 30% of P1 · GPS Impact: 12%
energy.energyAudit: Have you completed an energy audit?
energy.tip: Include solar panels, wind turbines, hydropower, or certified green energy tariffs.
energy.multipleBuildings: If you operate multiple buildings, enter the combined annual totals across all buildings and meters.
energy.noVehicle: No vehicle — walking, cycling, or non-motorised tours
energy.noFixedBase: We have no fixed office, workshop, or base of operations
energy.noFixedBaseDesc: Mobile operations — meet clients at trailheads, harbours, etc.
energy.officeElectricityHint: Building consumption only — do NOT include vehicle charging
energy.totalGasHint: If no gas: 0. 1 kg LPG = 13.9 kWh
energy.tariffRenewableDesc: Green tariff certificate from energy provider. If none: 0
energy.gridExportTooltip: Surplus renewable energy (solar, wind, etc.) fed back into the public grid. This reduces your net energy consumption and carbon footprint.
energy.gridExportHint: From your inverter or net metering bill. If none: 0
energy.evidenceSource: What is the source of this energy data?

water.title: Water
water.stepSubtitle: Indicator 1B · Weight: 25% of P1 · GPS Impact: 10%
water.tip: Water management is especially important in water-stressed destinations.
water.totalWaterHint: From water meter or utility bills. 1 m³ = 1 000 litres.
water.recirculationDesc: Each practice adds +3.3 points (max +9.9). Select all that apply.
water.evidenceSource: What is the source of this water data?

waste.title: Waste
waste.stepSubtitle: Indicator 1C · Weight: 20% of P1 · GPS Impact: 8%
waste.tip: Circular waste practices significantly reduce your environmental footprint.
waste.estimationTitle: How to estimate your annual waste:
waste.estimationBody: Most operators don't weigh waste daily — that's OK. Use one of these standard methods:
waste.bagCountDesc: Count bags per week × average bag weight (a standard 120L bin bag ≈ 8–12 kg) × 52 weeks.
waste.containerDesc: Number of bin pickups × container capacity (e.g. 240L wheelie bin ≈ 20 kg, 1100L skip ≈ 90 kg).
waste.invoiceDesc: Check your waste collection invoices for tonnage or volume data.
waste.estimationNote: If you don't know yet, leave it empty — you can track for a week and come back to fill it in.
waste.totalWaste: Total waste produced in the last 12 months (kg)
waste.totalWasteHint: All waste streams combined: general, recyclable, organic, hazardous
waste.breakdownIntro: Break down your {total} kg into the categories below. What's left ({remaining} kg) is assumed to go to landfill.
waste.recycledHint: Paper, plastic, glass, metal sent to recycling. Check with your waste collector for recycling tonnage.
waste.compostedHint: Food scraps, garden waste composted on or off site.
waste.otherDivertedHint: Donated, repurposed, or any other waste kept from landfill (textiles, electronics, construction debris).
waste.foodWasteProgramme: Active food waste reduction programme (+5 pts)
waste.evidenceSource: What is the source of this data?

carbon.title: Carbon
carbon.stepSubtitle: Indicator 1D · Weight: 15% of P1 · GPS Impact: 6%
carbon.emissionsTracked: Do you measure your carbon emissions?
carbon.tip: Tracking emissions is the first step toward reducing them.
carbon.autoCalcInfo: We auto-calculate your carbon emissions from the energy data you already provided. No extra input needed for Scope 1 & 2.
carbon.scope2Label: Scope 2 — Electricity (kWh × grid factor × (1 − green tariff %))
carbon.scope3Hint: Optional. Includes refrigerant leakage, business travel, waste transport, and guest commuting. Leave blank if you haven't measured this yet — it won't affect your score negatively.
carbon.scope3Body: Scope 3 is not included in your Carbon Intensity (1D) score — only Scope 1 & 2 count. This field is collected for transparency and future reporting. If you don't have this data, simply leave it blank.
carbon.scope3Sources: If you do track it, common sources include:
carbon.evidenceSource: What is the source of this data?

site.title: Site & Biodiversity
site.stepSubtitle: Indicator 1E · Weight: 10% of P1 · GPS Impact: 4%
site.tip: Protecting local ecosystems is a core part of regenerative practice.
site.environmentQuestion: How would you describe the natural environment surrounding your operation?
site.pristineNatural: Pristine natural — remote, minimal human impact
site.lowImpactNatural: Low-impact natural — mostly natural with limited infrastructure
site.mixed: Mixed — blend of natural and developed areas
site.mostlyDeveloped: Mostly developed — predominantly urban or built-up
site.fullyUrban: Fully urban — entirely developed, no natural surroundings

employment.title: Employment
employment.stepSubtitle: Indicator 2A · Staff composition, local hiring, contract types, and wages
employment.livingWage: Do you pay a living wage?
employment.tip: Local employment keeps value in the community.
employment.soloToggle: I am a solo operator (no employees)
employment.seasonalToggleDesc: Permanent contract % floored at 50% in scoring only
employment.soloSummaryDesc: Employment metrics default to 100% for solo operators. You can continue.
employment.totalFteTooltip: Sum of all staff hours / 40h week. Part-time = 0.5 FTE.
employment.localFteTooltip: Staff whose primary residence is within 100 km.
employment.localFteError: Local FTE cannot exceed total FTE.
employment.permanentContractTooltip: Include permanent staff + contracts longer than 6 months. Family members working regularly count as permanent even without formal contracts.
employment.familyRunBody: Family members who work regularly in the business count as permanent staff, even if they don't have formal employment contracts. Count them towards your permanent %.
employment.avgWageTooltip: Average across all local staff, gross before tax.
employment.minimumWageTip: Local minimum wage: €870/month (Portugal, 2024). The wage component reaches its maximum (50 pts) at 2× the country minimum wage benchmark (€1740/month).

procurement.title: Local Procurement
procurement.stepSubtitle: Indicator 2B · Weight: 30% of P2 · GPS Impact: 9%
procurement.tip: Local procurement reduces emissions and supports local economies.
procurement.infoBox: This data measures how much of your spending stays in the local economy. We compare local vs total spend to calculate your Local Procurement Rate — a key P2 indicator.
procurement.fbContext: You indicated {fsLabel} — tell us how much you spend on food & beverage and what % comes from local suppliers.
procurement.fbWarning: Local F&B spend cannot exceed total.
procurement.nonFbWarning: Local non-F&B spend cannot exceed total.

revenue.title: Revenue & Booking
revenue.stepTitle: How guests find and book you
revenue.stepSubtitle: Indicator 2C · Weight: 20% of P2 · GPS Impact: 6%
revenue.tip: Direct bookings mean more revenue stays with you and your community.
revenue.infoBox: Direct bookings keep more revenue in your business and signal a loyal guest base. Platforms are counted but weighted lower in the P2 score.
revenue.totalBookings: Total bookings in the last 12 months
revenue.totalBookingsTooltip: Count of completed reservations (arrivals) in the last 12-month period.
revenue.directBookingTooltip: Bookings made via your own website, phone, email, or walk-in — excluding any OTA or platform.
revenue.allDirectToggle: All our bookings are direct — no platforms
revenue.allDirectToggleDesc: Sets your direct booking rate to 100%
revenue.localOwnershipTooltip: Percentage of the business owned by residents within 100 km.
revenue.localOwnershipNote: Pre-filled from your business profile. Editable here.

community.title: Community
community.stepSubtitle: Indicator 2D · Weight: 15% of P2 · GPS Impact: 4.5%
community.communityInvestment: Do you invest in local community projects?
community.tip: Community investment strengthens your local integration score.
community.engagementQuestion: How would you describe your operation's engagement with the local community?
community.deeplyIntegrated: Deeply integrated — formal community access, cultural programming, 2+ local org partnerships
community.activeEngagement: Active engagement — regular participation in local events and community initiatives
community.moderatePresence: Moderate presence — some community activities or local sponsorships
community.minimalInteraction: Minimal interaction — limited to occasional local sourcing or referrals
community.noEngagement: No engagement — no meaningful community involvement

status.title: Regenerative Status
status.stepSubtitle: Pillar 3: Regenerative Contribution — 30% of your total score.
status.hasProgram: Do you have an active regenerative program?
status.tip: A regenerative program actively restores or improves the local ecosystem or community.
status.activePartner: Active programme with a partner organisation
status.activePartnerDesc: NGO, university, public body — full scoring
status.informalContributionsDesc: Full scoring, but traceability likely lower
status.forwardCommitment: Forward commitment — want to start, not yet active
status.forwardCommitmentDesc: P3 = 15 (partial credit). We'll match you with a local partner.

programme.title: Programme Details
programme.name: Program name
programme.tip: Programs with institutional partners score higher due to accountability.
programme.informalCapBody: Informal contributions are capped at 'Partially traceable' (50) on the Traceability dimension. To unlock higher Traceability scores, formalise your programme with an institutional partner (Status A or B).
programme.categoryTitle: Which contribution categories does your programme cover?
programme.traceabilityTitle: How independently verifiable is the impact?
programme.additionalityTitle: To what extent would this programme exist without you?
programme.continuityTitle: How established is this contribution programme?
programme.traceabilityFullyVerified: Fully verified — multiple institutions, peer-reviewed data
programme.traceabilityStrong: Strong — academic partner with signed impact reports
programme.traceabilityGood: Good — local NGO with signed documentation
programme.additionalityEntirelyDependent: Entirely dependent — would not exist without you
programme.additionalitySome: Some — financial contribution but would continue
programme.continuityLongTerm: Long-term — 3+ years with dedicated annual budget
programme.continuityGrowing: Growing — under 1 year, formal budget allocated
programme.continuityStarting: Starting — documented intention, very early stages
programme.continuityAdHoc: Ad hoc — no formal commitment or budget

forwardCommitment.title: Forward Commitment
forwardCommitment.stepSubtitle: Declare your commitment to activating a regenerative programme in a future cycle.
forwardCommitment.description: By signing, you commit to measurable improvement goals and gain 15 baseline points.
forwardCommitment.tip: The Forward Commitment signals intent to improve, even from a low baseline.
forwardCommitment.statusBody: Your P3 score will be 0 this cycle. Your GPS is computed from Pillar 1 and Pillar 2 only, renormalised to 0–100. A Forward Commitment Record will be created upon submission.
forwardCommitment.preferredCategoryHint: Which category fits your territory and operation best?
forwardCommitment.territoryContextHint: Describe the ecological, cultural, or scientific need in your territory. Max 200 words.
forwardCommitment.territoryContextPlaceholder: Why does this category fit your territory?
forwardCommitment.institutionTypeHint: What kind of institution do you intend to partner with?
forwardCommitment.targetCycleHint: Which assessment cycle do you commit to having an active partner?
forwardCommitment.signatoryHint: Full legal name of the person signing this commitment.

checklist.title: Evidence Checklist
checklist.stepSubtitle: Tick what you can provide — each document directly impacts your GPS.
checklist.subtitle: Upload supporting documents for your responses.
checklist.tip: Verified evidence improves your score tier from Declared to Verified.
checklist.missingEvidenceWarning: Missing evidence reduces your score by 19.5 points
checklist.documentsNote: Don't worry if you don't have everything now — you can upload from your dashboard at any time.
checklist.evidenceQualityNote: Evidence quality directly impacts your score.
checklist.evidenceQualityDetail: Each document is evaluated by tier: Primary (×1.00) — utility bills, invoices, receipts. Secondary (×0.75) — supplier certificates. Tertiary (×0.50) — self-reported. Proxy (×0.25) — no evidence provided.
checklist.uncheckedNote: Unchecked items default to proxy (×0.25). Check "Required tier" items to use your selected evidence quality, and "Detailed verification" items to boost further.

upload.stepSubtitle: Link supporting documents to this assessment. Evidence is optional — T3 files must be verified before P3 scores are published.
upload.noFilesDesc: Use Evidence Management to upload files, then return here to link them to this assessment.
upload.noFilesDescSuffix: to upload files, then return here to link them to this assessment.
upload.noSnapshotDesc: Evidence files can be added after your first submission. You may also pre-register them in Evidence Management.
upload.noSnapshotDescPrefix: Evidence files can be added after your first submission. You may also pre-register them in
upload.storagePathHint: Path or URL in your document storage.
upload.checksumHint: File integrity hash for audit trail.

delta.title: Year-on-Year Delta
delta.stepSubtitle: Compare this cycle's performance to your prior assessment.
delta.subtitle: Compare your performance to the previous cycle.
delta.tip: Showing improvement over time increases your credibility score.
delta.noPriorCycle: No prior cycle found. Delta comparison will be available from Cycle 2 onwards.
delta.majorChangesHint: Optional. Describe significant changes to operations, programmes, or infrastructure.
delta.majorChangesPlaceholder: e.g. Installed a 40 kW solar array, reducing energy intensity by ~30%. Added two new local F&B suppliers…
delta.dpsNote: The Delta Performance Score (DPS) is computed automatically from your locked Cycle 1 baseline. You do not need to calculate it manually.

gpsPreview.title: Your GPS Preview
gpsPreview.stepSubtitle: Cycle 1 score. Your Progress Modifier unlocks in Cycle 2 — up to +10 points for improvement.
gpsPreview.subtitle: This is your estimated Green Passport Score based on your responses.
gpsPreview.note: This is a preview. Your final score will be verified by our team.
gpsPreview.noDpiWarning: We do not yet have a DPI calculated for this destination. The current calculation uses Madeira as a reference territory, so the results may not fully reflect your local market.
gpsPreview.dpsTitle: How the Progress Modifier works (from Cycle 2)
gpsPreview.dpsBody: The Progress Modifier rewards year-over-year improvement, ranging from −5 to +10 points applied to your Performance Score. It's computed from three DPS components:
gpsPreview.dpsNote: The raw DPS total is converted to a bounded modifier: DPS × 0.4, clamped to [−5, +10].

submit.title: Submit your assessment
submit.stepSubtitle: Your preliminary Green Passport Score based on the data you provided.
submit.subtitle: Review your responses before submitting.
submit.tip: Once submitted, our team will review and verify your data within 5–7 business days.
submit.noDpiWarning: We do not yet have a DPI calculated for this destination. The current calculation uses Madeira as a reference territory, so the results may not fully reflect your local market.
submit.step1: Your data is saved and a preliminary score is calculated.
submit.step2: Upload supporting evidence from your operator dashboard.
submit.step3: Our team reviews and verifies your submission — typically within 5–7 business days.
submit.step4: Once verified, your score is published on your public profile.
submit.reviewTitle: Want to review your scores before publishing?
submit.reviewBody: After submitting, you'll receive a personalised action plan. You can also request a review call with our sustainability team — your score won't be published until you approve it.
submit.baselineWarning: This is your baseline assessment (Cycle 1). Future assessment cycles will measure your improvement trajectory through the Direction of Performance Score (DPS).
submit.declarationText: I confirm that the information provided is accurate and verifiable. I understand my score won't be published until evidence is reviewed.

success.title: Assessment submitted!
success.subtitle: Thank you for joining The Regenerative Tourism.
success.body: Our team will review your assessment within 5–7 business days. You'll receive an email once your Green Passport is verified and live.
success.heroTitle: Your Green Passport is on its way
success.heroBody: Your assessment has been received. Here's what happens next and how to make the most of your score.
success.uploadDesc: Head to your operator dashboard and upload supporting documents — utility bills, payroll records, supplier invoices. Evidence strengthens your score.
success.reviewDesc: A TRT reviewer will verify your data and evidence. You'll receive an email when review is complete.
success.goLiveDesc: Once verified, your score is published on your public Green Passport profile — visible to travellers who care.
success.rec1: Consider switching to a renewable energy tariff — this can significantly boost your P1 score.
success.rec2: Local procurement is one of the highest-impact P2 improvements you can make.
success.rec3: Formalising your community engagement programme can unlock P3 Status A.

dashboard.tagline: Your regenerative travel journey starts here

passport.title: Your Travel Passport
passport.description: Track your regenerative choices, earn impact credits, and discover verified operators who are making a difference.

evidence.title: Evidence Review
evidence.subtitle: Verify or reject operator evidence before GPS scores are published.

empty.allClear: No evidence pending review. All clear!

error.title: Dashboard unavailable
error.description: We couldn't load your operator dashboard right now.

inProgress.title: Your assessment is in progress
inProgress.description: Your progress has been saved. Pick up where you left off to complete your Green Passport.

operatorCode.label: Your Operator Code
operatorCode.hint: Display this code at your reception — travelers scan it to check in and earn points

publicProfile.title: Complete your public profile
publicProfile.description: Add your tagline, description, amenities, and languages so travelers can discover you.

score.proxyNote: Score calculated with Proxy multipliers (×0.25) until evidence is approved. Score updates automatically as each item is verified.

p1.mid: Moderate footprint. Renewable energy and water efficiency are key levers.
p1.low: High grid dependence. Significant improvement margin.

p2.high: Strong local integration across employment and procurement.
p2.mid: Local integration growing. Focus on direct bookings and community ties.

p3.high: Active regenerative programme with measurable outcomes.
p3.mid: Early-stage regenerative contribution. Formalise your programme.

territory.label: Territory Index — {name}
territory.dpiNote: Choosing a high-GPS operator in a high-pressure destination is the highest-impact booking choice a traveler can make.

dps.title: Directional Progress Score
dps.range: Rewards direction of travel — not just current position. Range: -10 to +25.

noAssessment.title: Your assessment is being processed.
noAssessment.description: Your submission has been received. Your Green Passport Score, pillar breakdown, and assessment details will appear here shortly.

evidence.title: Evidence
evidence.subtitle: Upload documents to verify your assessment data. Higher-tier evidence improves your score confidence.

help.title: Not sure what to upload?
help.description: Upload any document you have that supports your data. Our team reviews every submission and will contact you if we need more information.

p1_1a_energy_intensity.label: Energy Intensity
p1_1a_energy_intensity.hint: kWh per guest-night from utility bills or meter readings

p1_1a_renewable_pct.label: Renewable Energy %
p1_1a_renewable_pct.hint: Certificate from energy supplier or solar installation invoice

p1_1b_water_intensity.label: Water Intensity
p1_1b_water_intensity.hint: Litres per guest-night from water bills or meter readings

p1_1c_waste_diversion_pct.label: Waste Diversion
p1_1c_waste_diversion_pct.hint: Waste audit report or recycling service contract

p1_1d_carbon_intensity.label: Carbon Intensity
p1_1d_carbon_intensity.hint: Carbon footprint report or emissions calculation

p1_1e_site_score.label: Site & Land Use
p1_1e_site_score.hint: Site management plan or ecological assessment

p2_2b_local_fb_rate.label: Local F&B Sourcing
p2_2b_local_fb_rate.hint: Supplier invoices showing local food & beverage purchases

p2_2b_local_nonfb_rate.label: Local Non-F&B Sourcing
p2_2b_local_nonfb_rate.hint: Supplier invoices for local services and materials

p2_2c_direct_booking_rate.label: Direct Booking Rate
p2_2c_direct_booking_rate.hint: Booking channel report from PMS or OTA dashboard

p2_2d_community_score.label: Community Engagement
p2_2d_community_score.hint: Community programme documentation or meeting minutes

p3_3a_contribution_programme.label: Contribution Programme
p3_3a_contribution_programme.hint: Programme description, budget, and impact measurement

toast.noAssessment: No assessment snapshot found. Complete an assessment first.
